chunks.heading: Why is it important to keep the Fund updated on my marital status?
chunks.text: It is important to keep the Fund updated on your marital status to determine survivor benefit eligibility.

chunks.heading: How do I report a change in marital status while in active service?
chunks.text: During active service, you must report changes to your employer, who will then notify the Fund. You can verify your status via your annual pension statement in Member Self-Service.

chunks.heading: Who is responsible for reporting marital status changes after retirement?
chunks.text: Once retired, you are personally responsible for informing the Fund of any marital status changes.

chunks.heading: Which marriages or unions does the Fund recognize?
chunks.text: The Fund recognizes marriages and unions that are legally acknowledged in their respective jurisdictions and that confer pension rights.

chunks.heading: What are the eligibility rules for a spouse to receive a survivor's benefit?
chunks.text: Spousal benefits generally apply if you were married before separating from service and remained married until death. If you marry after separation, you must purchase an annuity within one year of the marriage for the new spouse to be eligible.

chunks.heading: How does the Fund handle divorce and multiple spouses?
chunks.text: The Fund may pay benefits to ex-spouses under specific circumstances, and can recognize more than one spouse if legally permitted in the jurisdiction of marriage.

chunks.heading: What is an important warning regarding the withdrawal settlement and survivor benefits?
chunks.text: Taking a withdrawal settlement upon separation negates all other entitlements, including survivor benefits.
